Pre-Market ALN Session Analysis

Asia (8PM–2AM ET) · London (2AM–8AM ET) · New York (8AM–4PM ET) · 10-Year Study · N = 4,448 days · Dec 2008 – Apr 2026

Total ALN Days
4,448
Dec 2008 – Apr 2026 · Pattern locked at 08:00 ET bar close
NY Broke Either Side
98.8%
Across all patterns. NY almost always expands beyond the London range.
Most Common Pattern
P3
London Partial Up · 37.4% of all days · 1,663 occurrences
Rarest Pattern
P2
Asia Engulfs London · Only 3.8% of days · 171 occurrences
Pattern Breakdown — All 4,448 Days · 10-Year Study
PatternDefinitionFreqnNY>LHNY<LLBoth SidesEither SideGiven LH→LLGiven LL→LH
P3 — London Partial Up London breaks Asia High only 37.4%1,663 79.8% 63.0% 44.0% 98.8% 55.1% 69.8%
P1 — London Engulfs Asia London breaks both Asia sides 30.2%1,345 71.0% 67.1% 39.9% 98.3% 56.1% 59.4%
P4 — London Partial Down London breaks Asia Low only 28.5%1,269 65.7% 74.5% 41.4% 98.8% 62.9% 55.6%
P2 — Asia Engulfs London London stays inside Asia range 3.8%171 76.0% 72.5% 49.1% 99.4% 64.6% 67.7%
NY>LH = broke London High · NY<LL = broke London Low · Given LH→LL = after taking high, prob of also taking low · Given LL→LH = after taking low, prob of also taking high
Pattern Deep Dive — Structure & Key Numbers

P3 — London Partial Up 37.4% · Most Common

London breaks Asia HIGH · London low stays inside Asia range · n=1,663
NY breaks London High (total)79.8%
NY breaks London Low (total)63.0%
NY breaks both sides44.0%
NY breaks either side98.8%
After LH taken → LL also taken55.1%
After LL taken → LH also taken69.8%
Primary setup: NY above London mid (70% of P3 days) → 84.5% London High breaks. Cleanest long bias in the dataset.

P4 — London Partial Down 28.5%

London breaks Asia LOW · London high stays inside Asia range · n=1,269
NY breaks London Low (total)74.5%
NY breaks London High (total)65.7%
NY breaks both sides41.4%
NY breaks either side98.8%
After LH taken → LL also taken62.9%
After LL taken → LH also taken55.6%
Mirror setup: NY below London mid (64% of P4 days) → 82.2% London Low breaks. Cleanest short bias in the dataset.

P1 — London Engulfs Asia 30.2%

London breaks BOTH Asia High AND Asia Low · n=1,345
NY breaks London High (total)71.0%
NY breaks London Low (total)67.1%
NY breaks both sides39.9%
NY breaks either side98.3%
After LH taken → LL also taken56.1%
After LL taken → LH also taken59.4%
Edge: NY above mid → 84.1% LH break. NY below mid → 83.0% LL break. After taking one side, only ~57% chance the other follows — take profits at first side.

P2 — Asia Engulfs London 3.8% · Rare

London trades entirely INSIDE Asia range · n=171
NY breaks London High (total)76.0%
NY breaks London Low (total)72.5%
NY breaks both sides49.1%
NY breaks either side99.4%
After LH taken → LL also taken64.6%
After LL taken → LH also taken67.7%
Edge: Highest both-sided rate (49.1%). NY above mid → 87.1% LH break. Below mid → 85.0% LL break. Use NY open position to pick direction — continuation is more likely than mean reversion once a side is taken.
NY 8AM Open vs London Midpoint Filter — All Patterns × All Days
The Midpoint Filter: London Midpoint = (London High + London Low) / 2. When the NY 8:00 AM open is above this level, London High break probability rises to 84–87% across ALL four patterns. When below, London Low break probability rises to 81–85%. The filter is symmetrical and directionally reliable regardless of which pattern is in play.
PatternTotal n NY8 Above MidnLH Break ↑LL Break ↓ NY8 Below MidnLH Break ↑LL Break ↓
P3 — London Partial Up 1,663 70.1%1,165 84.5%55.2% 29.7%494 68.6%81.2%
P1 — London Engulfs Asia 1,345 52.8%710 84.1%53.0% 47.2%635 56.4%83.0%
P4 — London Partial Down 1,269 35.8%454 85.0%60.4% 63.8%810 54.7%82.2%
P2 — Asia Engulfs London 171 40.9%70 87.1%54.3% 58.5%100 68.0%85.0%
LH Break = NY broke London High at any point during 8AM–4PM. LL Break = NY broke London Low. LH/LL break % are not mutually exclusive — both can occur on two-sided days.
🟢 Highest-Conviction LONG Setup
84.5%
P3 + NY opens ABOVE London Mid
n=1,165 days · London High breaks
Pattern frequency37.4% of all days
P3 days opening above mid70.1% of P3 days
London High break rate84.5%
London Low risk (downside)55.2% — near coin-flip
Broke high only43.3%
Broke both sides41.1%
Combined frequency: P3 on 37.4% of all days × 70.1% open above mid = this setup appears on ~26% of all trading days. Your primary long bias day.
🔴 Highest-Conviction SHORT Setup — Mirror Trade
82.2%
P4 + NY opens BELOW London Mid
n=810 days · London Low breaks
Pattern frequency28.5% of all days
P4 days opening below mid63.8% of P4 days
London Low break rate82.2%
London High risk (upside)54.7% — near coin-flip
Broke low only (no LH break)~41%
Broke both sides41.4%
Combined frequency: P4 on 28.5% of all days × 63.8% open below mid = this setup appears on ~18% of all trading days. Your primary short bias day.
P3 Deep Dive — London Partial Up × NY 8AM Midpoint (Full Split)
NY8 ABOVE LONDON MID · n=1,165 · 70.1% of P3 days
NY broke London High (total)84.5%
NY broke London Low (total)55.2%
Broke high only (no LL break)43.3%
Broke low only (no LH break)14.1%
Broke both sides41.1%
Broke either side98.6%
Trade: Long bias. London High is target. London Low break is 55% — use as stop reference, not a fade trigger.
NY8 BELOW LONDON MID · n=494 · 29.7% of P3 days
NY broke London High (total)68.6%
NY broke London Low (total)81.2%
Broke high only (no LL break)18.2%
Broke low only (no LH break)30.8%
Broke both sides50.4%
Broke either side99.4%
Trade: Even on P3 (bullish pattern), NY below mid flips to 81.2% London Low break. Don't fight the midpoint filter — it overrides the pattern direction.
Conviction Summary — NY Open vs London Midpoint × Pattern (Sorted by LH Break %)
RankPatternNY PositionnLH BreakLL BreakBoth SidesConviction
1 P2 — Asia Engulfs London Above Mid 70 87.1% 54.3% 49.1%* EXTREME
2 P4 — London Partial Down Above Mid 454 85.0% 60.4% 41.4%* HIGHEST
3 P3 — London Partial Up Above Mid 1,165 84.5% 55.2% 41.1%* HIGHEST
4 P1 — London Engulfs Asia Above Mid 710 84.1% 53.0% 39.9%* HIGHEST
5 P2 — Asia Engulfs London Below Mid 100 68.0% 85.0% 49.1%* EXTREME
6 P1 — London Engulfs Asia Below Mid 635 56.4% 83.0% 39.9%* HIGHEST
7 P4 — London Partial Down Below Mid 810 54.7% 82.2% 41.4%* HIGHEST
8 P3 — London Partial Up Below Mid 494 68.6% 81.2% 44.0%* HIGH
Key takeaway: The midpoint filter is so powerful it overrides the pattern direction. P4 (bearish pattern) opening ABOVE the London mid still yields 85.0% London High break — higher than most bullish patterns. P3 (bullish pattern) opening BELOW the mid yields 81.2% London Low break. Check the midpoint first. Every time. Without exception.
* Both-sides % shown is the pattern total (not split by midpoint position). NQ 1-min OHLCV · Dec 2008–Apr 2026 · N=4,448 · PHILBIGO Trading System.

NY Session Structure

Directional bias, pRTH analysis, and high/low formation timing

RTH Open vs Prior Day RTH Range
CASE 1

Opens OUTSIDE Prior Day RTH Gap Day

83.3% probability that price will NOT break the opposite side of pRTH. Use the opposite side as a hard resistance/support — fading moves toward it is statistically favored.

CASE 2

Opens INSIDE Prior Day RTH Inside Day

72.7% probability that at least one side of pRTH will break during RTH. Expect range expansion — bias toward whichever side NY open is closer to.

IB Direction Bias by Closing Position
IB closed in UPPER half of range: 81% probability the IB HIGH breaks first.
IB closed in LOWER half of range: 74% probability the IB LOW breaks first.
IB Break Timing
Broken before 12:00 PM
83%
Broken before 4:00 PM
96%
Session Level Hit Timing — All Levels (8AM–4PM NY Session)
Level Hit Rate Mean Hit Time Median Hit Time 25th Pct 75th Pct Signal
7–8 AM Midpoint 92.1% 08:27 08:15 08:02 08:32 NEAR CERTAIN
Asia High 78.8% 08:47 08:05 08:00 08:48 HIGH
London High 73.9% 09:13 08:36 08:28 09:18 HIGH
2–3 AM Midpoint 74.3% 09:03 08:35 08:25 09:05 HIGH
Asia Low 71.6% 08:51 08:29 08:00 08:56 MODERATE
Midnight Open 69.4% 09:10 08:37 08:28 09:16 MODERATE
London Low 67.8% 09:12 08:39 08:30 09:18 MODERATE
Key Insight: Most level touches happen early. The 75th percentile for all levels is still within the first 1–1.5 hours of the NY session (8AM open). Your 8AM–12PM window captures the large majority of level interactions.

Extension Touch ↑ Touch ↓ Close ↑ Close ↓ Close INSIDE Band Touch → Close (conditional)

IB60 Break Timing Window
10:30–11:59 ET
68% of all breaks · Both-sided: 36%
12:00–12:59 ET
9% · Both-sided: 20%
14:00+ ET
2% · One-dir ONLY · 131pt median
Hard Rule: Stop trading IB expansion setups at noon ET. 68% of the edge window closes before noon. Late breaks (14:00+) are exclusively one-directional and the day's range is mostly consumed.

Regression: RTH ≈ (IB60 × 1.48) + 69 pts  |  T1 = extreme ± (IB60 × 0.65)  |  T2 = extreme ± (IB60 × 1.22)

The Paradox — Touch Probability Increases with Distance
2AM Mid — Touch Rate by Excursion Distance
Excursion from MidTouch RateImplication
0–10 pts86%Price barely moved
10–20 pts93%Strong pull
20–30 pts94%Still very high
50–75 pts99%Exhaustion signal
100+ pts100%All returned
7AM Mid — Touch Rate by Excursion Distance
Excursion from MidTouch RateImplication
0–10 pts81%Price barely moved
10–20 pts87%Standard retrace
30–40 pts92%Still magnetic
80–100 pts100%Every day returned
150–200 pts100%Extreme mean rev.
The Paradox Rule: NEVER abandon a midpoint level because price moved far away. The further price moves from either mid, the higher the return probability. Extended moves represent exhaustion, not continuation. The only time to abandon: a strong one-directional London move with no retrace attempt for 2+ hours (the 6–10% of "trend days").
Confluence Setup — When Both Mids Are Active
ScenarioFrequencyAction
2AM mid untouched at 7AM ~15% of sessions Both 2AM AND 7AM mids become active targets. Strongest setup of the day.
2AM mid = 7AM mid (within 10 pts) Occasional EXTREME CONFLUENCE — two independent 90%+ probability levels stacked at same price. Day's primary target.
2AM mid touched, 7AM mid not ~75% of sessions Standard setup — only 7AM mid is active during your session. Use as primary intraday reference.
Both mids touched before 8AM Rare Both levels resolved. Look for next structural levels (session highs/lows, pRTH).
New Study · 17-Year Directional Edge
The 9:30 Open vs Midpoint Predicts Day Direction
4,400 NQ sessions · Dec 2008–Mar 2026 · Three midpoints tested + confluence trade
What's new: The 2AM and 7AM studies above measure touch probability. This new study measures directional edge — which side of each midpoint the 9:30 cash open lands on, and how strongly that predicts where the day closes. Three windows tested across 17 years (vs 6 years above): adds the wide London 2–8 AM full window, and quantifies the confluence trade when all three align.
Window A · London 2–8 AM
77.3%
Hit rate · 9:30 onward (Mode B)
Directional edge (above)+9.0 pp
Directional edge (below)+10.7 pp
Median pre-hit excursion18.4 pts
Median min to hit6 min
Role: Context level / middle ground
Window B · Asian 2–3 AM
+15.1 pp
Below-side edge · strongest in study
Directional edge (above)+13.6 pp
Directional edge (below)+15.1 pp
Hit rate (Mode B)71.0%
Median pre-hit excursion21.3 pts
Role: Directional bias filter
Window C · Pre-open 7–8 AM
80.9%
Hit rate · strongest magnet target
Directional edge (above)+8.7 pp
Directional edge (below)+9.0 pp
Mode A hit rate94.2%
Median pre-hit excursion15.8 pts
Role: Magnet target
Directional Prediction from 9:30 — Mode B (Cash Open Onward)
For each midpoint, the probability of closing on the same side as 9:30, versus the unconditional baseline. Edge = conditional probability − baseline probability in percentage points. The 9:30 cash open beats the 8:00 futures open as a directional read every time.
Midpoint n P(close above | 9:30 above) Edge above P(close below | 9:30 below) Edge below Baseline
London 2–8 AM 4,421 64.4% +9.0 pp 55.3% +10.7 pp 55.4%
Asian 2–3 AM STRONGEST 4,401 69.0% +13.6 pp 59.7% +15.1 pp 55.4%
Pre-open 7–8 AM 4,421 62.9% +8.7 pp 54.8% +9.0 pp 54.2%
The take: Hit rate decreases as the window moves earlier in the night; directional edge moves the opposite direction. The 2–3 AM midpoint is the highest-information bias filter available. The 7–8 AM midpoint is the highest-probability magnet target. The 2–8 AM London midpoint is both, moderately.
9:30 Confluence — When All Three Midpoints Align
The headline finding: When 9:30 prints above all three midpoints (36.5% of all days), the day closes above the London mid 66.9% of the time — a +11.5 pp edge that exceeds any single midpoint signal. When 9:30 is below all three (32.4% of days), the close lands below 59.0% — a +14.4 pp edge. Together this covers 69% of all sessions. Mixed states carry zero edge.
The Monotonic Edge Ladder
9:30 State n % of Sample P(close above London mid) Edge vs Baseline Conviction
3-above — above all three mids 1,606 36.5% 66.9% +11.5 pp LONG BIAS
2-above — above two of three 761 17.3% 60.3% +4.9 pp WEAK
1-above — mixed signals 607 13.8% 52.9% −2.6 pp NO EDGE
0-above — below all three mids 1,426 32.4% 41.0% +14.4 pp SHORT BIAS
Baseline (all sessions) 4,400 100% 55.5% — —
Clean monotonic step from −14.4 pp (all below) up to +11.5 pp (all above). The signal lives in the alignment — mixed states (1-of-3, 2-of-3) carry weak or no edge. Trade direction only when full alignment exists.
Joint Edge — When Confluence Predicts Closing Confluence
3-above → close above ALL three
61.9%
Baseline 49.0% · Edge +12.9 pp
0-above → close below ALL three
54.6%
Baseline 39.3% · Edge +15.3 pp
Recent Regime · May 2024 – May 2026 · The Edge Has Strengthened

68.3%
Edge +14.8 pp

63.2%

~75 pts
75th pct ~130 pts
Vol regime warning: 24mo median pre-hit excursion is ~75 pts vs ~18 pts all-time. Stop sizing must scale ~4× — a stop tighter than ~65 pts is at meaningful risk of being taken out on a typical successful midpoint pull. Right-size or skip the trade.
17-Year Stability — Confluence Edge by Year (3-above case)
Edge (in pp) of "9:30 above all three mids → close above London mid" vs the year's baseline. Positive every single year for 18 years; only one weak reading (2021 at +1.4 pp).
Year 3-above n % of year P(close↑ Lon) Baseline Edge
20098032.5%67.5%57.3%+10.2

Operational Rules — Which Midpoint for Which Job
01

Use Asian 2–3 AM mid as the directional filter

Strongest read in the study (+13.6 / +15.1 pp; +15.3 / +18.1 pp in last 24mo). If 9:30 prints above the 2–3 AM mid, the day closes above it 69% of the time. Use this as the binary "long bias / allow shorts" gate for the session.

02

Use 7–8 AM mid as the magnet target

80.9% hit rate Mode B, 94.2% Mode A. Use this as the primary objective for fade-to-mid entries from the 9:30 cash open — most touches occur within 30 minutes. Combines with the existing 90.1% touch-by-9:30 finding above.

03

Use 2–8 AM London mid as context

Middle-ground hit rate and edge. Use as an interim resistance/support reference between the 2–3 AM bias level and the 7–8 AM target. A 9:30 print far from London mid but close to 7–8 AM mid (or vice versa) is information about which side dominates.

04

Confluence trade is the highest-conviction trade

When 9:30 prints above (or below) all three midpoints simultaneously — 69% of all sessions — directional edge climbs to +11.5 / +14.4 pp all-time, +14.8 / +16.7 pp in last 24mo. Mixed states (1-of-3, 2-of-3) carry near-zero edge. Wait for full alignment before trading direction off this filter.

05

Skip if 9:30 is 80+ pts from your target mid

For Asian 2–3 AM: hit rate falls to 48.1% at this distance — the only sub-50% cell in the entire study. For pre-open 7–8 AM: 58.6%. These are gap or trend-day signatures — alignment with the 130 SMA matters more than reversion. Don't fade into extreme distance.

06

Size stops to current vol — ~75 pts not ~20 pts

Median pre-hit excursion in last 24mo is ~75 pts across the windows. All-time median was ~18 pts. A stop tighter than ~65 pts is at meaningful risk of being taken out on a typical successful day in current vol. Right-size or skip the play.

07

Use post-hit asymmetry on the second leg

Across all three midpoints: from below, hit-then-continue-through wins ~55%. From above, hit-then-revert-back wins ~54%. Net bullish skew supports letting long fades run further than short fades — consistent with FIERCE1's long-only default plus one discretionary short.

Methodology: NQ 1-min OHLCV, Dec 2008–Mar 2026, n≈4,400 sessions. Each midpoint = (High + Low) / 2 across its window. "Mode B" = NY session defined as 9:30 AM – 4:59 PM ET (cash open onward). "Hit" = any NY 1-min bar whose [low, high] contains the midpoint. Edge = P(matching close direction | matching open side) − unconditional P. Source timestamps Central; shifted +1h to ET. Days require ≥60 London bars (≥20 for single-hour windows), ≥150 NY bars, non-zero range.

Noon Curve Concept

20 years of data · NY Session 8:00 AM – 4:00 PM · High/low formation relative to 12:00 PM

Core Finding
74.3%
The session high and low form on opposite sides of 12:00 PM. One forms in the AM session, the other in the PM session.
Implication: If a clear high or low is established in the AM session, there is strong expectancy (74.3%) that the opposite extreme will form in the PM session. Trade the PM leg.
Quarterly Structure (2-Hour Blocks)
Q1 — 8:00–10:00 AM
Sets the AM high or low. 66% of PM-high days → Q1 set the AM low. 68% of PM-low days → Q1 set the AM high.
Q2 — 10:00–12:00 PM
If Q1 established both high and low, and Q2 breaks one of those levels — this signals the opposite extreme will be taken in PM. High-conviction signal.
Q3 — 12:00–2:00 PM
PM session begins. If AM established a clear extreme, expect PM to form the opposite. Noon is the structural pivot of the day.
Q4 — 2:00–4:00 PM
Closing session. Continuation of PM directional move or reversal toward close. Low statistical edge for new setups.
Noon Curve — Practical Trading Rules
RULE 1

Q1 Establishes the Extreme

If Q1 (8–10 AM) sets a clear high or low that holds into Q2, this becomes your "opposite side" target for the PM session. 66–68% of days, Q1 is where the AM extreme forms.

RULE 2

Q2 Break Confirms PM Direction

If Q2 breaks one of the Q1 extremes (both high and low were set in Q1), treat this as a signal that the opposite Q1 extreme will be taken during the PM session (Q3–Q4).

RULE 3

Trade the PM Leg at 12:00 PM

74.3% of days form their session high and low on opposite sides of noon. After noon, look for the PM directional move toward the opposite session extreme. Combine with IB60 targets for precise entries.

RULE 4

High/Low Wicks — Early/Late Candles

Swings formed in the first 20 minutes of the 9:00 AM hour are less likely to hold (pre-RTH volatility). Early and late segments of candles often form wicks; middle segments contain expansion. Don't trust pre-open spikes as session highs/lows.

💰 Account Baseline
Set a baseline date when starting a new funded account. Balance shown in the header counts only trades closed on or after this date. Trade history and stats are preserved.
⚡ Finnhub — Market News API
Free at finnhub.io — no country restrictions, 60 calls/min, real financial news. Key available instantly after signup.
Falls back to Claude web search automatically if no key set.
📡 ntfy.sh — Level Auto-Fetch
Zapier reads your TradingView alert email → posts to ntfy.sh → FIERCE1 fetches and fills all levels automatically. Set up your Zapier zap first, then enter your channel name below.
If Auto-Fetch fails, use 📋 Paste & Fill — copy the ntfy message then click the button in Morning Brief.
TradingView Alert Setup — 6 Daily Alerts
Set up all 6 alerts on NQ1! using the exact message formats below. Webhook URL for all: https://ntfy.sh/YOUR_CHANNEL
Alert 1 — 8:01 AM ET · Overnight Levels
ASH: {{plot_0}} ASL: {{plot_1}} LDH: {{plot_2}} LDL: {{plot_3}} NY Open: {{plot_4}} PDH: {{plot_5}} PDL: {{plot_6}}
Daily chart · bar close · fills all overnight levels + computes London Mid
Alert 2 — 9:30 AM ET · NY Open + Bias Confirm
NYOPEN: {{close}}
1-min chart · bar open · updates NY Open field + shows LONG/SHORT bias vs London Mid
Alert 3 — 10:00 AM ET · IB Forming
IBFORMING HOD: {{high}} LOD: {{low}}
1-min chart · bar close at 10:00 ET · tracks IB range building
Alert 4 — 10:30 AM ET · IB Close
IBCLOSE HOD: {{high}} LOD: {{low}} IBRANGE: {{plot_0}}
1-min chart · bar close at 10:30 ET · IB is set, confirms extension targets
Alert 5 — 12:00 PM ET · Noon Curve
NOON HOD: {{high}} LOD: {{low}} PRICE: {{close}}
1-min chart · bar close at 12:00 ET · triggers noon stop rule, captures AM extreme
Alert 6 — 4:00 PM ET · EOD Levels
UPDATE HOD: {{high}} LOD: {{low}} CLOSE: {{close}}
Daily chart · bar close · triggers EOD auto-grade of Strategy Lab setups

Your data lives in Supabase, not the browser. A local-only reset will reload from Supabase on next visit. Use Reset Local + Supabase for a true fresh start.
Reset Local + Supabase wipes all 4 tables permanently. Cannot be undone.
